name: Grisons (Switzerland)
description: A square flag combining the arms of the historic Three Leagues: a black-and-white per-pale field (Grey League), a quarter of blue and gold with a counterchanged cross (League of God's House), and a white field with a black ibex (League of the Ten Jurisdictions).
history: Adopted in 1933 to replace the earlier side-by-side display of the three leagues' arms; the modern design combines simplified versions of the historic coats of arms.

features.role: shield tierced, combining the arms of the Three Leagues
features.symbolism: the union of the Grey League, the League of God's House and the League of the Ten Jurisdictions

features.role: quarterly blue and gold with a cross counterchanged (League of God's House)
